creator: geodni@free.fr
text:
I'm using a new computer "Lenovo ThinkCentre A60" with an AMD Athlon 64 X2 3800+. I successfully used it for 2 months but I still cannot get rid of the "missing drivers" for RAM at boot time: 
pci0: <memory, RAM> at device 0.0 (no driver attached)

The workstation works wery well, applications like OpenOffice.org 2.1 launches in 5 seconds.

Also but not so important, the onboard video and sound chip are not recognised. I successfully use this computer as a workstation under Xorg 6.9 on a 19" wide screen 1440x900 after a special recompilation of the nvidia drivers from XFree86. I tried all sound drivers available from /boot/kernel/snd_*.ko with no success. Video onboard card is recognised as a "GeForce 6100" by Xorg.

I provide some outputs of "/boot/loader.conf", "/var/run/dmesg.boot" and "pciconf -lv". I can provide more information if needed.

/boot/loader.conf :
==================
ichsmb_load="YES"


/var/run/dmesg.boot :
